The user is showing me an image with three icons (keyboard, accessibility/person, and speaker/audio icons) and wants them moved to the lower right of the viewport. 

I need to:
1. First understand what this is - it appears to be related to the OOBE (Out of Box Experience) for Surface project
2. Find where these icons are defined in the HTML/CSS
3. Modify the CSS to position them in the lower right

Let me search for these accessibility icons in the codebase. They look like they could be in the main index.html or in one of the page files. Let me search for icon-related code and positioning.

I should look at the index.html and the CSS files to understand the current structure.I'll help you move those icons to the lower right of the viewport. Let me first check the current HTML and CSS to see how these icons are positioned. Now I can see the structure. The accessibility icons are in `.oobe-below-actions` which is currently positioned with `margin: 16px 0 0` and `justify-content: flex-end`. To move them to the lower right of the viewport, I'll need to position them absolutely or fixed. Let me update the CSS: Done! The icons have been moved to the lower right of the viewport. I changed the positioning from relative to fixed with `bottom: 24px` and `right: 24px`, which will keep them anchored to the lower right corner of the viewport regardless of scrolling. The icons now have a `z-index: 1000` to ensure they stay on top of other content.
